data.title: Dai-Ichi Life: May Repurchase Up to 5.4% of Outstanding Shares
Acciona Energia Total Capacity At 15,045 MW At End-March
data.description:
Corporacion Acciona Energias Renovables SA :

  • SAID ON WEDNESDAY TOTAL CAPACITY AT END-MARCH 15,045 MW

  • TOTAL PRODUCTION AT END-MARCH 7,408 GWH

  • AVERAGE GENERATION PRICE AT END-MARCH 72.0 EUROS/MWH

  • INSTALLED WIND CAPACITY AT END-MARCH 10,458 MW

  • TOTAL WIND PRODUCTION AT END-MARCH 6,040 GWH

  • MAINTAINS 2025 EBITDA FROM OPERATIONS TARGET AND EBITDA FROM ASSET ROTATION TARGET

  • OUTLOOK FOR CAPACITY TO BE INSTALLED BY YEAR-END IS REDUCED TO ABOUT 0.6 GW FOLLOWING TEMPORARY PAUSE OF TWO BATTERY STORAGE PROJECTS IN US

  • REDUCES INVESTMENT TARGET BEFORE ASSET ROTATION TO ABOUT 1.3 BILLION EUROS

  • INVESTMENT DECISIONS IN USA MAY VARY ACCORDING TO OUTCOME OF TARIFF NEGOTIATIONS

  • COMMITTED INVESTMENT FOR 2026 REMAINS AT MODERATE LEVELS

  • CURRENTLY IMPLEMENTS STRATEGY FOCUSED ON PRESERVING BROAD FINANCIAL FLEXIBILITY AND MAINTAINING ITS ABILITY TO ADAPT TO POTENTIAL CHANGES IN MARKET CONDITIONS

Source text:

Further company coverage:

data.title: Agility Public Warehousing to Halt Trading in Dubai
data.description:
Agility Public Warehousing (DFM:AGLTY) will temporarily pause trading on the Dubai Financial Market from 2:30 pm local time on Thursday due to a board meeting.

The Dubai-listed supply chain services company's shares will restart trading after it publishes its meeting results, according to a same-day release.

data.title: Engie 1Q Ebitda Excl. Nuclear Power EUR4,9B
Engie 1Q EBITDA EUR5.4B
Engie 1Q Cash Flow From Operations EUR4.0B
Hitachi Energy India's Profit Rises in Fiscal Q4; Shares Down 5%
data.description:
Hitachi Energy India posted an increase in its profit to 1.84 billion Indian rupees in the fiscal fourth quarter ended March 31, against 1.14 billion rupees a year ago.

Earnings per share grew to 43.15 rupees compared with the year-ago figure of 26.82 rupees, according to a Wednesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges by the energy solutions provider.

Revenue from operations in fiscal Q4 also rose to 18.8 billion rupees from 17.0 billion rupees a year earlier.

The company's board recommended a final dividend of 6 rupees per equity share of face value of 2 rupees each.

Hitachi's shares were down over 5% in recent trade.

data.description:

data.title: KBC to buy Slovakia's 365.bank for 761 million euros
data.description:
Belgian bank KBC Groep said on Thursday it has reached an agreement to acquire 98.45% of Slovakia-based 365.bank for 761 million euros ($851.7 million).

The bank said the acquisition will help strengthen its presence in Slovakia and the Central and Eastern Europe region.

"Our goal has always been and remains to strengthen our presence in Central and Eastern Europe ... Through this acquisition, we strengthen our geographical diversification (and) we continue to build market leadership in Slovakia," KBC's CEO Johan Thijs said in a statement.

Thijs had told Reuters in February that he was keen to expand his business in this area.

The transaction would have an effect of about 50 basis points on KBC's CET1 ratio, which stood at 14.5% in the first quarter as the bank reiterated its ambition to be amongst the best-capitalised financial institutions in Europe.

($1 = 0.8935 euros)

data.title: TSMC Approves Capital Appropriations Worth $15.3 Billion
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing , or TSMC, approved capital appropriations totaling $15.3 billion for acquiring machinery equipment and real estate.

The allocations include $9.6 billion for advanced technology machinery, $308 million for advanced packaging and specialty technology equipment, and $5.4 billion for real estate and capitalized leased assets.

The purchases will be made from multiple suppliers worldwide, according to a Tuesday Taiwan Exchange filing.

The procurements will support the chipmaker's production capacity and operational needs, it added.

data.title: Epsilon to Raise AU$1 Million via Loan Notes Issuance
data.description:
Epsilon Healthcare (ASX:EPA) said it will raise AU$1 million via the issue of up to 50 million loan notes at AU$0.02 per note, to sophisticated, experienced, or professional investors, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The notes, issued in tranches, carry a 10% annual interest and mature on June 30, the filing said.

Subject to shareholder approval, they may be converted into fully paid ordinary shares at the noteholder's election, the filing added.

Proceeds will fund service expansion, working capital, and new product development at Epsilon's manufacturing facility, the company said.

data.title: Engie Logs Growth in Q1 Revenue; Outlook Affirmed
data.description:
Engie said Thursday its revenue for the first quarter increased year over year.

Revenue for the three months ended March 31 was 23.3 billion euros, compared with 22 billion euros a year ago.

The French utility affirmed its outlook for full-year 2025, including a net recurring income group share in the range of 4.4 billion euros to 5 billion euros.

data.title: Market Chatter: Former Unisplendour Chair Gets Death Sentence Over Corruption, Embezzlement
data.description:
A Chinese court sentenced Zhao Weiguo, ex-chair of semiconductor company Unisplendour , to death with a two-year reprieve for corruption and embezzlement, Reuters reported Wednesday.

Zhao, who diverted company funds for personal gain and harmed a listed firm's interests, was fined 12 million yuan, according to the report.

The sentence could be commuted to life imprisonment if no further crimes are committed, Reuters wrote.

data.title: India's Muthoot Finance drops as Kotak flags near-term challenges
data.description:
** India's Muthoot Finance slumps 5.7% to 2,131 rupees

** Gold loan financier beat fourth-quarter profit estimates but Kotak analysts flagged near-term risks for the lender

** Reserve Bank of India (RBI) proposed a loan-to-value (LTV) ratio limit of 75% which would apply throughout the loan tenure and not just at origination

** A cut in LTV will pose a risk to margins and growth, says Kotak

** Moreover, with high growth, competition in the sector is likely to increase, Kotak says citing IIFL Finance resuming gold loans and Bajaj Finance's strong gold loan growth

** Brokerage downgrades MUTT to "reduce" from "add" with fair value at 2,275 rupees from 2,250 rupees

** Avg rating on MUTT at "buy"; median PT is 2,500 rupees - data compiled by LSEG

** Stock trims YTD gains to 0.4%

data.title: New Zealand Stocks Rise on Thursday
data.description:
New Zealand's benchmark S&P/NZX 50 index rose 0.8% to close at 12,881 on Thursday, driven by gains in health technology stocks.

Heavyweights Fisher & Paykel and Ebos Group advanced 3.7% and 0.5%, respectively.

Other large-cap stocks also ended higher, including Auckland Intl Airport (1.3%), Mercury NZ (1.5%), Contact Energy (0.9%), and A2 Milk (1.3%).

Investor sentiment was further buoyed by a temporary halt in the trade war between the US and China, which eased worries of a global recession.

In domestic news, annual food inflation in New Zealand rose to 3.7% in April, the highest in fifteen months, from 3.5% in March.

data.title: Bridgestone Maintains Revenue, Net-Profit Views for 2025
CEZ proposes dividend at top of range, first-quarter profit above estimates
data.description:
Czech electricity producer CEZ proposed paying a 47 crown ($2.11) per share dividend on Thursday, amounting to 80% of last year's profit and below a previous payout of 52 crowns.

The dividend was at the top of the company's typical payout ratio of 60% to 80% of adjusted net profit. CEZ shares had closed on Wednesday at 1,194 crowns and have risen 30% in the past year.

CEZ earlier this year reported a 9% decrease in 2024 adjusted net profit to 31.8 billion crowns.

On Thursday, it confirmed its outlook for 2025 and expects an adjusted net profit of between 25 billion crowns and 29 billion crowns.

CEZ said its first-quarter adjusted net profit fell 6% to 12.7 billion crowns, affected by higher depreciation and amortisation. The average estimate in a Reuters poll had seen adjusted profit at 10.4 billion crowns in the quarter.

Earnings before interest, tax, depreciation and amortisation (EBITDA) rose 7% year-on-year in the quarter to 43 billion crowns.

CEZ slightly increased its full-year EBITDA outlook to between 127 billion crowns and 132 billion crowns in 2025, versus 137.5 billion crowns in 2024, as lower electricity prices weighed.

This year is likely the last in which CEZ will pay a windfall tax that was introduced after energy price spikes following Russia's invasion of Ukraine in 2022.

CEZ said it had pre-sold around two-thirds of its expected 2026 generation output at an average 94 euros per MWh, versus an average realised price of 120 euros to 125 euros assumed for 2025.

($1 = 22.2570 Czech crowns)

data.title: CTBC Bank Redeems Structured Bonds Early
data.description:
CTBC Financial unit CTBC Bank triggered an early redemption event on its nine-month USD knock out fixed coupon basket equity-linked structured bonds.

The bonds were repaid following a knock-out event, according to a Taiwan Exchange filing on Wednesday.

data.title: Rich Capital Completes Disposal of Subsidiary
data.description:
Rich Capital Holdings completed the disposal of its entire stake in wholly owned subsidiary, Rich Batam, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

In an earlier filing, the company had entered into an agreement with Yi Chengshu to dispose of the subsidiary for an aggregate consideration of SG$1.

Accordingly, Rich Batam has ceased to be a subsidiary of Rich Capital Holdings.

data.title: Bridgestone 1Q Americas Adjusted Operating Profit Y39.79B Vs Y36.8B a Year Earlier
Yida China Logs 151 Million Yuan in April Contracted Sales
data.description:
Yida China recorded contracted sales of around 151 million yuan in April, a Wednesday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

Gross floor area stood at approximately 13,112 square meters, while the average selling price was 11,530 yuan per square meter.

From January to April, contracted sales and GFA reached roughly 323 million yuan and 27,733 square meters, respectively, with an average selling price of 11,654 yuan per square meter.
